questions: Based on the U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics Occupational Outlook Handbook data for 2024, identify all specific occupations within the 'Computer and Information Technology' group, as well as related 'Computer and Information Systems' management roles, that reported a 2024 median annual wage exceeding $130,000. For each occupation meeting this salary threshold, provide the exact 2024 median pay, the typical entry-level education required, and a brief summary of the job duties.

rounds.answer_a:
Based on the U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics' most recent data (May 2023), the following occupations within the Computer and Information Technology field reported a median annual wage exceeding $130,000.

**Computer and Information Systems Managers**
These managers earn a median annual wage of $169,510. A bachelor's degree is the typical entry-level education, often coupled with related work experience. Their primary duties involve planning, coordinating, and directing an organization's computer-related activities. They analyze IT needs, recommend and implement technological upgrades, and ensure the security of the organization's network and electronic data [1].

**Computer and Information Research Scientists**
With a median pay of $145,080 per year, these professionals typically need a master's degree. They invent and design new approaches to computing technology and find innovative uses for existing technology. Their work involves developing and improving software systems and exploring fundamental questions in computing to solve complex problems in business, science, and medicine [2].

**Computer Network Architects**
The median annual salary for this role is $132,600, requiring a bachelor's degree for entry. Also known as network engineers, they design and build data communication networks, including local area networks (LANs), wide area networks (WANs), and intranets. They create network models, analyze traffic, and implement security measures [3].

**Software Developers, Quality Assurance Analysts, and Testers**
Earning a median annual wage of $130,160, these professionals generally hold a bachelor's degree. Software developers design and build computer programs and applications. Quality assurance analysts and testers focus on identifying problems with software before it is released to the public, creating test plans, and documenting any issues found [4].

## References
[1] U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics. (2024, April 17). *Occupational Outlook Handbook, Computer and Information Systems Managers*. Retrieved from https://www.bls.gov/ooh/management/computer-and-information-systems-managers.htm
[2] U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics. (2024, April 17). *Occupational Outlook Handbook, Computer and Information Research Scientists*. Retrieved from https://www.bls.gov/ooh/computer-and-information-technology/computer-and-information-research-scientists.htm
[3] U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics. (2024, April 17). *Occupational Outlook Handbook, Computer Network Architects*. Retrieved from https://www.bls.gov/ooh/computer-and-information-technology/computer-network-architects.htm
[4] U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics. (2024, April 17). *Occupational Outlook Handbook, Software Developers, Quality Assurance Analysts, and Testers*. Retrieved from https://www.bls.gov/ooh/computer-and-information-technology/software-developers.htm
rounds.answer_b:
Computer and Information Research Scientists – 2024 median annual wage: $145,080. Entry-level education: Master’s degree. These scientists invent and improve computing theories, algorithms, and models; design experiments to test new software or hardware approaches; and work with scientists and engineers to solve complex data-intensive problems across fields such as artificial intelligence, robotics, and bioinformatics [1].

Computer and Information Systems Managers (also called IT or IS managers) – 2024 median annual wage: $173,670. Entry-level education: Bachelor’s degree. They plan, coordinate, and direct an organization’s computer-related activities, determine information technology goals, implement the hardware and software needed to meet those goals, supervise technical staff, negotiate with vendors, and ensure data security and efficient network operations [2].

No other occupations in the Bureau of Labor Statistics’ 2024 Occupational Outlook Handbook Computer and Information Technology group reported median pay above $130,000; Computer Network Architects ($126,900), Software Developers ($124,200), and Information Security Analysts ($120,360) fall below the specified threshold. Thus, only the two roles above meet or exceed the $130,000 benchmark for 2024.
rounds.judge_reasoning: Agent A is the clear winner as it uses the most current available data (May 2023, published 2024) to correctly identify all four relevant occupations that meet the salary threshold, including Software Developers and Network Architects. Agent B relies on outdated data (likely 2022) and explicitly excludes two valid occupations (Developers and Architects) claiming they are below the threshold, which is factually incorrect for the 2024 reporting period. Agent A also provides a proper reference list, whereas Agent B includes citation markers but fails to provide the actual references.
